exemplar.title:
Which applicator for lip gloss between the brands of Clinique, Clarins and Moira is best suited
for me and my friends by picking up the most product and still being able to apply with ease to
our lips?

subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Clear, descriptive title that states focus and methods
Effective table of contents outlining major sections
Use of headings and figures aids basic navigation
weaknesses: Stated page count conflicts with actual pages, disrupting organization
Frequent narrative digressions and repeated statements reduce conciseness
Inconsistent figure numbering and misplacement of subâsections interrupt logical flow
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the exploration has a basic structure and some coherence, significant issues with organization, repetition, and irrelevant material limit coherence and conciseness to the lowest band meriting only 1 / 4.

strengths: Use of standard mathematical notation for integrals and areas in most places
Inclusion of hand sketches, photographs, and GeoGebra graphs as multiple representations
Shape approximations in figures effectively support the mathematical models
weaknesses: Axis scales and units are often missing on graphs and figures
Inconsistent notation (e.g. omission of Ï in calculations) affects precision
Weak transitions and lack of inline figure references reduce clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates some appropriate use of mathematical language and multiple representations with overall understandable communication, but inconsistencies and missing details prevent a higher band, justifying 2 / 4.

strengths: Topic choice and firsthand measurements show significant independent thinking
Personal context (lip-gloss brands) clearly drives the exploration
Initiative demonstrated in original data collection enhances engagement
weaknesses: Mathematical treatment remains elementary with limited novel techniques
Creativity is confined to context selection rather than extending methods
Further personal reflection on the impact of results is minimal
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: There is clear personal motivation and evidence of independent initiative, placing the work in band 2 for personal engagement; the limited mathematical creativity prevents the top band, so 2 / 3 is awarded.

strengths: Discussion paragraph briefly comments on limitations
Some attempt to reflect on integrals and continuous change
weaknesses: Reflection is largely descriptive with limited depth
Critical analysis of modeling assumptions and error propagation is minimal
Connections to deeper mathematical understanding remain superficial
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection is present but only at a basic, descriptive level with limited critique and weak linkage to understanding, meriting only 1 / 3.

strengths: Area and volume calculations use relevant SL-level mathematics
Segmentation of the wand and use of symmetry for integrals shows sound modeling
Awareness of measurement uncertainty (Â±0.5 mm) adds critical evaluation
weaknesses: Several setup errors (radius vs. diameter, limits of integration) affect correctness
Units are omitted in intermediate steps and Ï is inconsistently applied
Explanation of underlying calculus principles and model choice is limited
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration applies appropriate SL mathematics with some correct results, but partial errors and minimal theoretical justification constrain the work to band 2, yielding 2 / 6.

strandSelections.reasoning: Choosing a personally relevant topic and deciding to model applicators independently shows "significant evidence" of the studentâs own thinking, even though the mathematics applied is elementary.

The student shows basic understanding of area formulas, gradients and solids of revolution, but explanation of why particular models are chosen and of underlying calculus principles is minimal.  This demonstrates "some understanding" rather than good or thorough understanding.

Numerical answers are plausible, yet several set-ups misuse radius/diameter, limits and units; rounding is inconsistent.  With partial correctness the work meets band 2 ("mathematics is partially correct").

Numerous repetitions (the aim is restated three times, formulas already provided are re-introduced) and lengthy personal narrative dilute the mathematical core.  Irrelevant market-size statistics and brand commentary further reduce conciseness, so the strand fits the second band.

Standard notation for integrals, gradients and areas is used and mostly correct (Ï omitted occasionally, units sometimes missing), so the work meets "some appropriate" language and notation but falls short of consistent accuracy.
Sentences are understandable but punctuation and unit use are inconsistent, and paragraph transitions are weak.  Overall communication is "mostly clear with some consistency."

Area and volume calculations are relevant and within the SL syllabus.  The modelling remains at a basic level with straight-line segments and a semicircle; thus band 2 ("some relevant mathematics at appropriate level") is justified rather than higher bands which require effective or sophisticated use.

The student includes hand sketches, photographs and GeoGebra graphs; these qualify as multiple representations.  Yet scales, axes and labels are partial, and some shapes are only verbally referenced.  Hence the strand sits in band 2 ("some appropriate representations").
Reflection is present mainly in the discussion paragraph, where the student briefly comments on limitations.  The comments are descriptive and limited, matching band 1 ("limited reflection present").

Apart from selecting the product context, there is little novelty in the mathematical treatment (basic area formulas and standard solids of revolution).  Creativity or initiative beyond syllabus techniques is therefore only "some."

The exploration follows the table-of-contents sequence and ideas generally progress from data collection to geometry, to calculus, to discussion.  However, the logical thread is frequently interrupted by narrative digressions or missing links (e.g. why halfâcross-sections are suddenly rotated for volume).  This indicates "some coherence and logical development" rather than a smooth, convincing line of argument.

The analysis of results is mainly a restatement of numerical comparisons; there is little critique of accuracy, modelling assumptions or error propagation.  This corresponds to "basic analysis with limited critique."

Connections to mathematical understanding are superficial: the student states integrals are useful because shapes change "continuously" but does not elaborate.  Hence only a "limited connection to understanding" is demonstrated.

Sections are clearly labelled and the student supplies figures and headings, so a basic structure is evident.  Still, sub-sections are sometimes misplaced (method inside introduction) and the bibliography appears on page 19 although the page count is stated as 18.  These inconsistencies keep the work at an "adequate organisation" level.

The work is clearly driven by the studentâs experiences with lip-gloss; measurements were collected firsthand and design choices are explained.  This represents a "clear personal approach that drives the exploration."

annotations.comment: The decision to segment the wand into simple shapes for area before using integrals is well justified.
The title clearly states the focus and methods, providing an effective overview of the explorationâs aim.
The stated page count (18) conflicts with the bibliography appearing on page 19. Ensure these match for accurate organization.
The table of contents effectively outlines sections, but consider adding sub-subsection page numbers for even clearer navigation.
Figure captions are inconsistently numbered (âFigure 1â, then âFigure 2â for wand). Standardize numbering for clarity.
Photographs alongside sketches show effective use of multiple representations but axis labels on GeoGebra graphs are missing.
The explanation for choosing half-cross-sections is briefly mentioned but lacks justification of the rotational model.
The uncertainty notation âÂ±0.5 mmâ displays awareness of measurement error, enhancing critical evaluation.
Including the bibliography on page 19 in your contents shows attention to completeness, supporting overall structure.
Methodology is listed under introduction yet spans pages 4â9. Consider moving the section header to the correct location for coherence.
The aim statement clearly identifies the brands and objective, showing relevance to personal experience.
The research question reiterates the aim almost verbatim; consider refining to avoid repetition and enhance conciseness.
The personal justification is vivid, demonstrating clear engagement and authentic context for the investigation.
The market-size statistic adds interest but dilutes mathematical focus; consider reducing or relocating this narrative.
The method description shows significant initiative in data collection through direct measurement.
When referencing figures, include labels inline (e.g. âsee Figure 1â) to reinforce multiple representations and clarity.
The shape approximations in Figures 1â3 are effective representations, aiding visual understanding of the models.
Graphing only half the profile is a valid simplification with integrals; this decision shows appropriate understanding of symmetry.
Include axis scales and units on Figures 7â8 to improve clarity and allow independent verification of volumes.
The integral formulas are correctly stated but Ï is occasionally omitted in calculationsâverify notation consistency.
The distinction between two integral formulas demonstrates insight into choosing appropriate models for different axis positions.
When introducing $$y=mx+c$$ and gradient formulas, connect explicitly to subsequent line-model integrations.
Restating the purpose of area calculations mid-section repeats earlier text; streamline to maintain conciseness.
The area formula application for the trapezoid of Cliniqueâs wand is correct but units are missing in intermediate steps.
Explicitly state how the half-area approach affects total calculation to clarify rationale behind doubling.
